title: Preterm Labor
chief_complaint: I'm 31 weeks pregnant. I've been having contractions every 5 minutes for the past 2 hours. My back hurts and I feel pressure in my pelvis. I had a preterm birth before at 34 weeks.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Uterus: Regular contractions q4-5min, moderate intensity, palpable. Fundal height 31cm. Fetal HR 150, reassuring.
physical_exam.Pelvic: Sterile speculum: No pooling, no ferning (no PPROM). Cervix: 3cm dilated, 80% effaced, vertex at -2 station. Positive fetal fibronectin swab (if obtained).
physical_exam.Fetal: Cephalic presentation. Estimated fetal weight 1,700g on bedside US.

hidden_data.cbc: WBC 12,500 (normal leukocytosis of pregnancy), Hb 11.5, Platelets 200,000
hidden_data.urinalysis: Normal. No nitrites, no leukocytes. Culture pending.
hidden_data.fetal_fibronectin: Fetal fibronectin positive (predicts increased risk of preterm birth within 2 weeks)
hidden_data.gbs: GBS unknown (culture pending - obtained at 35-37 weeks normally)

correct_diagnosis: Preterm Labor at 31 Weeks with Cervical Dilation (3cm) and Positive Fetal Fibronectin
key_learning_points: Preterm labor = regular contractions causing cervical change between 20-37 weeks. Preterm birth = leading cause of neonatal morbidity and mortality. Accounts for 10% of all births

Risk factors: Prior preterm birth (strongest - 20% recurrence), Short cervix (<25mm on TVUS at 18-24 weeks), Multiple gestation, Uterine anomalies, Cervical insufficiency, Infection (UTI, chorioamnionitis, bacterial vaginosis), PPROM, Polyhydramnios, Stress, Smoking, Cocaine, African-American race

Diagnosis: Regular contractions (>4 in 20min or >8 in 60min) + Documented cervical change (dilation, effacement) OR Cervix ≥2cm dilated and ≥80% effaced. Fetal fibronectin: Negative = <1% chance of delivery within 2 weeks (good rule-out test). Cervical length >30mm = unlikely preterm labor

Management: 1) Tocolysis (delay delivery 48h for steroids to work - does NOT improve neonatal outcomes beyond 48h): Nifedipine 20mg PO then 10-20mg q6-8h (first-line - calcium channel blocker). Indomethacin 50-100mg PO/PR loading then 25-50mg q6h ×48h (NSAID - second-line, avoid after 32 weeks - premature ductus arteriosus closure, oligohydramnios). Terbutaline 0.25mg SC q20-30min (beta-agonist - third-line, maternal cardiac effects limit use). MgSO4 (no longer recommended for tocolysis - ONLY for neuroprotection)

2) Antenatal corticosteroids: Betamethasone 12mg IM q24h ×2 doses (or Dexamethasone 6mg IM q12h ×4 doses). 24-34 weeks. Reduces RDS (50%), IVH (50%), NEC, and neonatal mortality. Optimal if delivery >24h and <7 days after first dose

3) Magnesium sulfate for neuroprotection: 4g IV loading over 20min then 1g/hr infusion. 24-32 weeks. Reduces cerebral palsy (30-40%). Indicated if delivery anticipated within 24h. Monitor for toxicity (loss of reflexes, respiratory depression, hypotension). 4) Antibiotics: GBS prophylaxis (Penicillin or Ampicillin) if GBS positive or unknown. NOT for routine prophylaxis in preterm labor without PPROM (no benefit)

Contraindications to tocolysis: Cervix >6cm (too late), Chorioamnionitis, Non-reassuring fetal status, Severe preeclampsia/eclampsia, Fetal demise, Lethal fetal anomaly, Maternal contraindication (significant hemorrhage, cardiac disease)
